steps.title: Link temperature and size
steps.content: The assertion says flies collected in cooler months should tend to have shorter life spans. The passage explains the link: wing centroid size is a proxy for body size, and body size correlates with life span. So the needed data must connect a cooler month with a smaller wing centroid size.

steps.title: Use the relevant comparison
steps.content: A compares female wing size in May with female wing size in June, so it links the cooler month to smaller wing size. B compares males with females rather than months. C gives only temperature and no wing-size evidence. D compares male wing sizes but does not use the female comparison that directly illustrates the stated finding. The answer is A.
